title: SL’s sorry plight - who is to blame?
body_lines: - The nation is yet to hear an apology from any leader for their mistakes.

- On March 16, when President Gotabaya Rajapaksa addressed the nation while the Treasury was scraping to find foreign exchange and at the peak of the power crisis, what stuck to mind was his refusal to take responsibility for any of his weak decisions.

- Then we had another Rajapaksa - the one dubbed as a 'Kaputa' who political sources said took over the Finance portfolio forcefully from his brother Mahinda Rajapaksa, thinking he could be the savior of the economy

- "This crisis was not created by me. When those who contributed to the creation of this crisis are criticizing the government in front of the people today, I am attempting to immediately resolve this crisis" - GR

- Then we had another Rajapaksa - the one dubbed as a 'Kaputa' who political sources said took over the Finance portfolio forcefully from his brother Mahinda Rajapaksa, thinking he could be the savior of the economy. But his failures and weak decisions were no better than the rest
- Basil denied he had failed to slow Sri Lanka’s descent into financial turmoil saying he was the person to send the first letter to the IMF
- Sajith Premadasa who was on multiple occasions requested to take the Premiership and take the country out of crisis, today only hurls criticisms at the government
Current Prime Minister Ranil Wickremesinghe, showing a gloomy picture of Sri Lanka's immediate future presently is also no 'Mr. Clean'

Sri Lanka is a country that has fallen like no other. A country which was once dubbed as the 'Miracle of Asia', 'Pearl Of the Indian Ocean', 'Investment Hub', 'Must See Destination', is today pleading with open hands, literally begging for fuel, medicines, food, essentials in front of the global community.

Whoever can help us, we plead with them. Whatever global agency that even inquires about our status, we rush to them. So is our desperation, to ensure that 22 million people can continue living, with at least the basics so that each day can pass.

For months, Sri Lankans have been surviving in fear. Every household has only one story to tell - when will a gas cylinder arrive in their home? When can they get some much-needed medicines? When can they buy milk powder and other essentials for their children? When can they fill their fuel and how long are the queues? And when will the power normalize? The fear now haunting Sri Lankans is unimaginable. But it is something they are compelled to now live with.
So how did this once-blooming hub turn into such a desperate failed state?

Who is the blame? Where did we go wrong? While the answer is straight, that mismanagement, theft, corruption, fraud, bribes, commissions, selfishness, greed, nepotism and misuse of power by our so-called politicians and public officials resulted in the suffering of an entire nation, it is not surprising that even to date none of these politicians and officials have accepted their faults and taken responsibility. The nation is yet to hear an apology from any leader for their mistakes.

On March 16, when President Gotabaya Rajapaksa addressed the nation while the Treasury was scraping to find foreign exchange and at the peak of the power crisis, what stuck to mind was his refusal to take responsibility for any of his weak decisions. Staring into the camera he read his written script with no regret in his eyes - "This crisis was not created by me. When those who contributed to the creation of this crisis are criticizing the government in front of the people today, I am attempting to immediately resolve this crisis and provide relief to the people," he said, clearly forgetting that his appointments to public office, his decisions, and his utter organic fertilizer disaster had anything to do with the crisis.

Forgetting that he too is now a politician after being elected to the highest seat in the country, he went on to say that in a crisis situation like this, it was the responsibility of the politicians and intellectuals of a country to collectively find solutions to the issues. Here again, he forgot that he was a President who had all powers under the 20th Amendment.

Former Prime Minister Mahinda Rajapaksa did no better than his brother when in a televised address to the nation a month later in April, he appealed to the country and protesters to have “patience” and said that the crisis-hit country was losing precious dollars because of protests on the streets.

Still with no apology whatsoever for his government's utter failures and his own, as he had served as the Finance Minister not too long ago, the Prime Minister atl east accepted that no matter how honourable the notion of organic fertilizer was, it was not the time for it to be implemented. However his decisions thereafter seemed to be ignored as his own brother, the President began a campaign to oust him thinking that if he did so, his failures would be covered up well and forgotten. On May 9, Mahinda Rajapaksa ultimately had to go, after deadly violence which claimed nine lives.

Then we had another Rajapaksa - the one dubbed as a 'Kaputa' who political sources said took over the Finance portfolio forcefully from his brother Mahinda Rajapaksa, thinking he could be the saviour of the economy. But his failures and weak decisions were no better than the rest. With the economy only blundering further, in a recent media briefing when he finally resigned from Parliament much to the relief of the protesters, Basil had no regret too and in fact went on to say that he was not passing the ball onto anyone else but the little fuel the country was still receiving was because of him. This one time MP denied he had failed to slow Sri Lanka’s descent into financial turmoil saying he was the person to send the first letter to the IMF after becoming finance minister. "It is the work I started that is now being taken forward,” he said. “I have no regrets.”

Shame for this senior politician to say this even while families in Hambantota, which holds the Rajapaksa legacy, and all other districts of the country, go to bed hungry amid the economic failures and skyrocketing cost of living.

Then in an interview with a foreign media agency this month, President Rajapaksa confirmed he will not resign and will complete his 5 years in office clearly ignoring the majority’s sentiments of ‘Gota Go Home’.

"I can't go as a failed President," he said but admitted his government had waited too long to seek help from the International Monetary Fund. "If we had gone at least six months or a year earlier, it would not have come to this state," he said. But still no apology as the Head of State to his 22 million citizens including the 6.9 million who voted for him.

The ruling MPs and public officials were no better, from blaming the media, to the public, to the protesters, the ball kept passing. But did they feel they were to blame? No, forgetting that it was they who were elected to office.

In fact even to this date, the only talk we hear from all those in government and who were in office, was that the blame was always on someone else. But they were never the fault. And now their solution to the public - use fuel sparingly, use electricity sparingly, eat less, grow your own food, you will have to wait for medicines, no dollars, limitations on spending overseas, no essential food especially for children, a sky rocketing cost of living, no gas and the worst is yet to come. It then boils down to the public having to sacrifice every step of the way without any accountability, or answers as to who is to blame.

The opposition also does not have a better story. SJB Leader Sajith Premadasa who was on multiple occasions requested to take the Premiership and take the country out of crisis, today only hurls criticisms at the government. He and his party, some senior members who have now broken away from him and his party, spend their days taking to social media and arriving in Parliament just to highlight the faults and insist on the President to go home despite Gotabaya Rajapaksa making it clear he would complete his term. These opposition members were not too long ago in government too, so washing their hands completely away from this crisis cannot do. It is only common sense that this severe crisis has stemmed from the mismanagements of previous governments as well and worsened at the hands of the Rajapaksas, who took the already frail economy into the grave.

Current Prime Minister Ranil Wickremesinghe, showing a gloomy picture of Sri Lanka's immediate future presently is also no 'Mr. Clean'. Although he had the courage to take over the country as the Prime Minister at a time when the nation is on boiling point, should remember that he was also the Prime Minister in the previous government when the 'Bond Scam' cost the Treasury millions. However whether he can bring relief to the nation when relief is needed the most is yet to be seen.

Sri Lanka's politicians have long tested the patience of its citizens. While bribery, corruption, nepotism, fraud have long plagued the country's political circle and Parliament, there seems to be no immediate hope as the country keeps depending on the same faces to take us forward. Whether the current public protests will change the system entirely is yet to be seen, if the protesters themselves keep the infiltrators with ulterior hidden motives away, stop harassing those who should not be targetted and do not fall for geo-political agendas and echo only in one voice - "We want change." (JAMILA HUSAIN)

si.title: ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ කණගාටුදායක තත්ත්වය - වරද කාටද?
body_lines: - තම වැරදි සඳහා කිසිදු නායකයෙකුගෙන් සමාව ඉල්ලීමක් ජාතිය තවමත් අසා නැත.

- මාර්තු 16 වෙනිදා, විදේශ විනිමය සොයා ගැනීමට භාණ්ඩාගාරය හූරමින් සිටියදී සහ විදුලි අර්බුදයේ උච්චතම අවස්ථාව වන විට, ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂ ජාතිය අමතන විට, ඔහුගේ මතකයේ රැඳුණේ ඔහු ගත් දුර්වල තීරණයක වගකීම භාර ගැනීම ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කිරීමයි.

- එතකොට අපිට තව රාජපක්ෂ කෙනෙක් හිටියා - දේශපාලන ආරංචි මාර්ග කියනුයේ ‘කපුටා’ නමින් හැඳින්වූ තැනැත්තා ආර්ථිකයේ ගැලවුම්කරුවා විය හැකි යැයි සිතා තම සොයුරු මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂගෙන් බලහත්කාරයෙන් මුදල් අමාත්‍යධුරය ලබා ගත් බවයි.
- "මේ අර්බුදය මම නිර්මාණය කළේ නැහැ. මේ අර්බුදය ඇති කිරීමට දායක වූ අය අද ජනතාව ඉදිරියේ රජය විවේචනය කරන විට මම මේ අර්බුදය වහා විසඳා ගැනීමට උත්සාහ කරනවා" - ජී.ආර්.

- එතකොට අපිට තව රාජපක්ෂ කෙනෙක් හිටියා - දේශපාලන ආරංචි මාර්ග කියනුයේ ‘කපුටා’ කියලා නම් කරපු එකා ආර්ථිකයේ ගැලවුම්කාරයා වෙන්න පුළුවන් කියලා හිතාගෙන මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ අයියාගෙන් බලහත්කාරයෙන් මුදල් ඇමැතිකම ගත්තා කියලා. නමුත් ඔහුගේ අසාර්ථකත්වයන් සහ දුර්වල තීරණ අනෙක් ඒවාට වඩා හොඳ නොවීය
- ශ්‍රී ලංකාව මුල්‍ය කැළඹීමට පත් වීම මන්දගාමී කිරීමට තමා අපොහොසත් වූ බව බැසිල් ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කළේ IMF වෙත පළමු ලිපිය යැවූ පුද්ගලයා තමන් බව පවසමිනි.
- අගමැතිකම අරගෙන රට අර්බුදයෙන් ගොඩ ගන්න කියලා අවස්ථා කිහිපයකදීම ඉල්ලීම් කළ සජිත් ප්‍රේමදාස අද ආණ්ඩුවට එල්ල කරන්නේ විවේචන පමණයි.
වත්මන් අග්‍රාමාත්‍ය රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහ, ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ වර්තමාන අනාගතය පිළිබඳ අඳුරු චිත්‍රයක් පෙන්වමින් සිටින්නේ ද 'මහත්තයා නොවේ. පිරිසිදු'

ශ්‍රී ලංකාව වෙන කවරදාටත් වඩා පහත වැටුණු රටකි. ‘ආසියාවේ ආශ්චර්යය’, ‘ඉන්දියන් සාගරයේ මුතු ඇටය’, ‘ආයෝජන මධ්‍යස්ථානය’, ‘නැරඹිය යුතුම ගමනාන්තය’ ලෙස කලක් විරුදාවලිය ලත් රටක් අද දෑතින්ම කන්නලව් කරන්නේ ඉන්ධන, බෙහෙත්, ආහාර, අත්‍යවශ්‍ය ද්‍රව්‍ය යනාදියයි. ගෝලීය ප්රජාව ඉදිරියේ.

අපට උදව් කළ හැකි අය, අපි ඔවුන්ගෙන් අයැද සිටිමු. අපේ තත්ත්‍වය ගැන පවා විමසන ගෝලීය ආයතනය කුමක් වුවත් අපි ඔවුන් වෙත ඉක්මන් වෙමු. අපගේ බලාපොරොත්තු සුන්වීම ද එසේමය, සෑම දිනකම ගෙවිය හැකි අවම වශයෙන් මූලික කරුණු සමඟින් මිලියන 22 ක ජනතාවකට දිගටම ජීවත් විය හැකි බව සහතික කිරීම.

මාස ගණනාවක් තිස්සේ ශ්‍රී ලාංකිකයන් ජීවත් වන්නේ බියෙනි. සෑම නිවසකටම කීමට ඇත්තේ එකම කතාවකි - ගෑස් සිලින්ඩරයක් තම නිවසට පැමිණෙන්නේ කවදාද? ඔවුන්ට අවශ්‍ය ඖෂධ ලබා ගත හැක්කේ කවදාද? තම දරුවන්ට කිරිපිටි සහ අනෙකුත් අත්‍යවශ්‍ය ද්‍රව්‍ය මිලදී ගත හැක්කේ කවදාද? ඔවුන්ට ඉන්ධන පිරවිය හැක්කේ කවදාද සහ පෝලිම් කොපමණ කාලයක් තිබේද? සහ බලය සාමාන්‍යකරණය වන්නේ කවදාද? දැන් ශ්‍රී ලාංකිකයන් හොල්මන් කරන බිය සිතාගත නොහැකි තරම්ය. නමුත් එය ඔවුන්ට දැන් ජීවත් වීමට බලකර ඇති දෙයකි.
එසේ නම් වරක් පිපෙන මෙම මධ්‍යස්ථානය මෙතරම් මංමුලා සහගත අසාර්ථක රාජ්‍යයක් බවට පත් වූයේ කෙසේද?

වරද කාගේද? අපිට වැරදුනේ කොතනද? පිළිතුර සෘජු වුවත්, අපේ ඊනියා දේශපාලනඥයන් සහ රාජ්‍ය නිලධාරීන්ගේ වැරදි කළමනාකරණය, සොරකම, දූෂණය, වංචාව, අල්ලස්, කොමිස්, ආත්මාර්ථකාමීත්වය, කෑදරකම, ඥාති සංග්‍රහය සහ බලය අවභාවිතය නිසා මුළු මහත් ජාතියක්ම දුක් විඳීමට සිදු වූ බව පුදුමයක් නොවේ. අද වනතුරුත් මේ කිසිම දේශපාලනඥයෙක් සහ නිලධාරියෙක් තමන්ගේ වැරදි පිළිගෙන වගකීම භාරගෙන නැහැ කියලා. තමන් කළ වැරදිවලට ​​කිසිදු නායකයකුගෙන් සමාව ඉල්ලීමක් ජාතියට තවම අසන්නට ලැබී නැත.

විදේශ විනිමය සොයා ගැනීමට භාණ්ඩාගාරය හූරමින් සිටියදී සහ විදුලි අර්බුදයේ උච්චතම අවස්‌ථාවේදී ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්‍ෂ මහතා ජාතිය අමතා මාර්තු 16 වැනිදා ජාතිය අමතන විට මතකයේ රැඳුණේ තමන් ගත් දුර්වල තීරණයක වගකීම භාර නොගැනීමයි. කැමරාව දෙසට නෙත් යොමා ඔහු ලියූ පිටපත කියෙව්වේ කිසිඳු පසුතැවිල්ලක් නැති දෑස්වලිනි - “මේ අර්බුදය මම නිර්මාණය කළ එකක් නොවේ, මේ අර්බුදය ඇති කිරීමට දායක වූ අය අද ජනතාව ඉදිරියේ රජය විවේචනය කරන විට, මම උත්සාහ කරන්නේ මේ අර්බුදය වහා විසඳා ජනතාවට සහන සලසන්න.’’ ඔහු ප‍්‍රකාශ කළේ ඔහු රාජ්‍ය තනතුරුවලට පත්කිරීම්, ඔහුගේ තීරණ සහ සම්පූර්ණයෙන්ම කාබනික පොහොර ව්‍යසනය අර්බුදයට සම්බන්ධ බව පැහැදිලිවම අමතක කරමිනි.

රටේ ඉහළම පුටුවට තේරී පත්වී තමාද දැන් දේශපාලනඥයකු බව අමතක කරමින් ඔහු වැඩිදුරටත් කියා සිටියේ මෙවැනි අර්බුදකාරී අවස්ථාවක රටේ දේශපාලනඥයින්ගේ සහ බුද්ධිමතුන්ගේ වගකීම වන්නේ සාමූහිකව විසඳුම් සෙවීම බවයි. ගැටලු. මෙහිදී නැවතත් ඔහුට අමතක වී ඇත්තේ තමන් 20 වැනි සංශෝධනය යටතේ සියලු බලතල හිමි ජනාධිපතිවරයකු බවය.

හිටපු අගමැති මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ මහතා මසකට පසු අප්‍රේල් මාසයේදී ජාතිය අමතා රූපවාහිනියෙන් ජාතිය අමතමින් “ඉවසිල්ලෙන්” සිටින ලෙස රට සහ විරෝධතාකරුවන්ගෙන් ආයාචනා කළ විට හිටපු අගමැති මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ තම සහෝදරයාට වඩා හොඳ දෙයක් කළේ නැත, අර්බුදකාරී රටට වටිනා ඩොලර් අහිමි වන නිසා වීදිවල විරෝධතා.

වැඩි කලකට පෙර මුදල් අමාත්‍යවරයා ලෙස කටයුතු කර ඇති බැවින්, තම ආණ්‌ඩුවේ සම්පූර්ණ අසාර්ථකත්වයන් සහ තමාගේම අසාර්ථකත්වයන් පිළිබඳව තවමත් සමාව නොගැනීමෙන්, කාබනික පොහොර පිළිබඳ සංකල්පය කෙතරම් ගෞරවනීය වුවත්, එය කාලය නොවන බව අග්‍රාමාත්‍යවරයා පිළිගත්තේය. එය ක්රියාත්මක කිරීම සඳහා. ඉන් පසු ඔහු ගත් තීරණ ඔහුගේම සහෝදරයෙකු ලෙස නොසලකා හැර ඇති බවක් පෙනෙන්නට තිබුණද, එසේ කළහොත් ඔහුගේ අසාර්ථකත්වය හොඳින් වසන් කර අමතක වනු ඇතැයි සිතා ජනාධිපතිවරයා ඔහුව එළවීමේ ව්‍යාපාරයක් ආරම්භ කළේය. මැයි 9දා අවසානයේ මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂට යන්නට සිදුවූයේ ජීවිත නවයක් බිලිගත් මාරාන්තික ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වයකින් පසුවය.

ඊට පස්සේ අපිට තවත් රාජපක්ෂ කෙනෙක් හිටියා - දේශපාලන ආරංචි මාර්ග කියන ‘කපුටා’ කියන එකා ආර්ථිකයේ ගැලවුම්කාරයා වෙන්න පුළුවන් කියලා හිතාගෙන තමන්ගේ සහෝදර මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂගෙන් බලහත්කාරයෙන් මුදල් ඇමැතිකම ගත්තා. නමුත් ඔහුගේ අසාර්ථකත්වයන් සහ දුර්වල තීරණ අනෙක් ඒවාට වඩා හොඳ නොවීය. ආර්ථිකය තව තවත් අවුල් වීමත් සමඟ පසුගියදා පැවති මාධ්‍ය හමුවකදී විරෝධතාකරුවන්ට සහන සලසමින් පාර්ලිමේන්තුවෙන් ඉල්ලා අස්වූ අවස්ථාවේදී බැසිල් ද පසුතැවිලි නොවූ අතර ඇත්ත වශයෙන්ම තමන් පන්දුව පාස් කළේ වෙන කාටවත් නොවන බවත් කියා සිටියේය. ඔහු නිසා තවමත් රටට ලැබුණේ සුළු ඉන්ධනයකි. වරක් මෙම මන්ත්‍රීවරයා ශ්‍රී ලංකාව මූල්‍ය කැළඹීමට පත් වීම මන්දගාමී කිරීමට අපොහොසත් වූ බව ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කළේ මුදල් අමාත්‍යවරයා වූ පසු IMF වෙත පළමු ලිපිය එවූ පුද්ගලයා තමන් බව පවසමිනි. “මම ආරම්භ කළ වැඩ තමයි දැන් ඉදිරියට ගෙන යන්නේ,” ඔහු පැවසුවේ “මට කිසිම පසුතැවිල්ලක් නැහැ.”
රාජපක්‍ෂ උරුමය හිමි හම්බන්තොට ඇතුළු රටේ සියලුම දිස්‌ත්‍රික්‌කවල පවුල් ආර්ථීක කඩාවැටීම් සහ ඉහළ යන ජීවන වියදම මධ්‍යයේ බඩගින්නේ යහනට යද්දීත් මේ ජ්‍යෙෂ්ඨ දේශපාලනඥයා මෙසේ කීම ගැන ලැජ්ජයි.

ඉන්පසුව මේ මාසයේ විදේශ මාධ්‍ය ආයතනයක් සමඟ සම්මුඛ සාකච්ඡාවකට එක්වෙමින්, ජනාධිපති රාජපක්ෂ තහවුරු කළේ තමා ඉල්ලා අස් නොවන බවත්, 'ගෝඨා ගෙදර යන්න' යන බහුතරයේ හැඟීම් පැහැදිලිවම නොසලකා හරිමින් සිය 5 වසර ධුර කාලය සම්පූර්ණ කරන බවත්ය.

"මට අසාර්ථක ජනාධිපතිවරයෙකු ලෙස යන්න බැහැ," ඔහු කී නමුත් තම රජය ජාත්‍යන්තර මූල්‍ය අරමුදලෙන් උදව් ලබා ගැනීමට බොහෝ කාලයක් බලා සිටි බව පිළිගත්තේය. “අපි අඩුම තරමින් මාස හයක් හෝ අවුරුද්දකට කලින් ගියා නම් මේ තත්ත්වයට එන්නේ නැහැ. එහෙත් තවමත් ඔහුට ඡන්දය දුන් මිලියන 6.9 ක් ඇතුළු මිලියන 22 ක පුරවැසියන්ගෙන් රාජ්‍ය නායකයා ලෙස සමාව ඉල්ලා නැත.
පාලක මන්ත‍්‍රීවරුන් සහ රාජ්‍ය නිලධාරීන් මාධ්‍යයට, මහජනතාවට, විරෝධතාකරුවන්ට දොස් පවරමින් බෝලය නොනවත්වා ඉදිරියට ගියහ. නමුත් ඔවුන් වරදකරු බව ඔවුන්ට හැඟුණාද? නැහැ.

ඇත්ත වශයෙන්ම අද දක්වාම, ආණ්ඩුවේ සහ තනතුරු දැරූ සියලු දෙනාගෙන් අපට ඇසෙන එකම කතාව වූයේ දොස් පැවරීම සැමවිටම වෙනත් කෙනෙකුට බවයි. නමුත් ඔවුන් කිසි විටෙකත් වරදක් කළේ නැත. දැන් මහජනතාවට ඔවුන්ගේ විසඳුම - ඉන්ධන අරපිරිමැස්මෙන් භාවිතා කරන්න, විදුලිය අරපිරිමැස්මෙන් භාවිතා කරන්න, අඩුවෙන් කන්න, ඔබේම ආහාර වගා කරන්න, ඔබට බෙහෙත් සඳහා බලා සිටීමට සිදුවනු ඇත, ඩොලර් නැත, පිටරට වියදම් කිරීමට සීමාවන්, විශේෂයෙන් ළමයින්ට අත්‍යවශ්‍ය ආහාර නැත, අහස රොකට් ජීවන වියදම, ගෑස් නැති අතර නරකම දේ තවම පැමිණ නැත. ඉන් පසු මහජනතාවට කිසිදු වගවීමකින් තොරව සෑම පියවරක්ම කැප කිරීමට සිදුවනු ඇත, නැතහොත් වරද කාගේදැයි යන්නට පිළිතුරු සපයයි.

විපක්ෂයටත් මීට වඩා හොඳ කතාවක් නැහැ. අගමැතිකම අරගෙන රට අර්බුදයෙන් ගොඩ ගන්න කියලා අවස්ථා කිහිපයකදීම ඉල්ලීම් කළ SJB නායක සජිත් ප්‍රේමදාස අද ආණ්ඩුවට එල්ල කරන්නේ විවේචන පමණයි. ඔහු ඇතුළු ඔහුගේ පක්‍ෂය, දැන් ඔහුගෙන් සහ ඔහුගේ පක්‍ෂයෙන් කැඩිලා සිටින සමහර ජ්‍යෙෂ්ඨයන් සමාජ මාධ්‍ය ජාලාවලට ගොස් පාර්ලිමේන්තුවට පැමිණ දින ගෙවන්නේ වැරදි මතුකර ගැනීමටත්, ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්‍ෂ පැහැදිලිව කීවත් ජනාධිපතිවරයාට ගෙදර යන්නැයි බලකර සිටීමටත්ය. ඔහුගේ වාරය සම්පූර්ණ කරනු ඇත. මේ විපක්ෂයේ මන්ත්‍රීවරුන් වැඩි කලකට පෙර ආණ්ඩුවේ සිටි අය නොවන නිසා මේ අර්බුදයෙන් සම්පූර්ණයෙන්ම ඉවත් වී අත් සේදීම කළ නොහැක. මේ උග්‍ර අර්බුදය පසුගිය රජයන්හි ද වැරදි කළමනාකාරීත්වයෙන් පැන නැඟී ඇති අතර දැනටමත් බිඳ වැටුණු ආර්ථිකය වළට දැමූ රාජපක්ෂලා අතින් තවත් නරක අතට හැරී ඇති බව සාමාන්‍ය බුද්ධියකි.

වත්මන් අග්‍රාමාත්‍ය රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහ, ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ වර්තමාන අනාගතය පිළිබඳ අඳුරු චිත්‍රයක් පෙන්වමින් සිටින්නේ ද 'මහත්තයා නොවේ. පිරිසිදු'. ජාතියම උතුරා යන මොහොතක අගමැති ලෙස රට භාර ගැනීමට තරම් ධෛර්යයක් එතුමාට තිබුණත්, ‘බැඳුම්කර මගඩිය’ මහා භාණ්ඩාගාරයට කෝටි ගණන් වියදම් වූ පසුගිය ආණ්ඩුවේ ද ඔහු අගමැති වූ බව මතක තබා ගත යුතුය. කෙසේ වෙතත් සහන අවශ්‍ය වන විට ඔහුට ජාතියට සහනයක් ලබා දිය හැකිද යන්න තවමත් දැකගත නොහැක.

ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ දේශපාලකයන් බොහෝ කලක සිට එහි පුරවැසියන්ගේ ඉවසීම පරීක්ෂා කර ඇත. අල්ලස, දූෂණය, ඥාති සංග‍්‍රහය, වංචාව දිගු කලක් තිස්සේ රටේ දේශපාලන කවය සහ පාර්ලිමේන්තුව පීඩාවට පත් කර ඇති අතර, අපව ඉදිරියට ගෙන යාමට රට එකම මුහුණු මත රඳා පවතින බැවින් ක්ෂණික බලාපොරොත්තුවක් නොමැති බව පෙනේ. පවතින මහජන විරෝධතා ක්‍රමය මුළුමනින්ම වෙනස් කරයිද යන්න තවමත් දැකගත නොහැක, විරෝධතාකරුවන් විසින්ම යටි සැඟවුණු චේතනා ඇති රිංගවන්නන් ඈත් කරන්නේ නම්, ඉලක්ක නොකළ යුතු අයට හිරිහැර කිරීම නවත්වා භූ දේශපාලන න්‍යාය පත්‍රවලට නොවැටී දෝංකාර දෙයි. එක හඬ - "අපට වෙනසක් අවශ්‍යයි." (ජමිලා හුසේන්)

ta.title: SL இன் பரிதாப நிலை - யாரைக் குறை கூறுவது?
body_lines: - தேசம் இதுவரை எந்தத் தலைவரிடமும் தங்கள் தவறுகளுக்கு மன்னிப்பு கேட்கவில்லை.

- மார்ச் 16 அன்று, ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ச நாட்டு மக்களுக்கு அன்னியச் செலாவணியைக் கண்டுபிடிக்க திறைசேரியைத் துடைத்தபோதும், மின்சார நெருக்கடியின் உச்சக்கட்டத்தில் இருந்தபோதும், அவரது பலவீனமான முடிவுகளுக்குப் பொறுப்பேற்க மறுத்ததுதான் மனதில் பதிந்தது.

- பின்னர் எங்களிடம் மற்றொரு ராஜபக்ச இருந்தார் - 'கபுடா' என்று அழைக்கப்பட்டவர், பொருளாதாரத்தின் மீட்பராக இருக்க முடியும் என்று நினைத்து, தனது சகோதரர் மஹிந்த ராஜபக்ஷவிடம் இருந்து நிதி இலாகாவை வலுக்கட்டாயமாக கைப்பற்றினார் என்று அரசியல் வட்டாரங்கள் கூறுகின்றன.

- "இந்த நெருக்கடி என்னால் உருவாக்கப்படவில்லை, இந்த நெருக்கடியை உருவாக்க பங்களித்தவர்கள் இன்று மக்கள் முன்னிலையில் அரசாங்கத்தை விமர்சிக்கும்போது, ​​இந்த நெருக்கடியை உடனடியாக தீர்க்க முயற்சிக்கிறேன்" - ஜி.ஆர்.

- பின்னர் எங்களிடம் மற்றொரு ராஜபக்ச இருந்தார் - 'கபுடா' என்று அழைக்கப்பட்டவர், அவர் பொருளாதாரத்தின் மீட்பராக இருக்க முடியும் என்று நினைத்து அவரது சகோதரர் மஹிந்த ராஜபக்ஷவிடம் இருந்து நிதி இலாகாவை வலுக்கட்டாயமாக கைப்பற்றினார் என்று அரசியல் வட்டாரங்கள் கூறுகின்றன. ஆனால் அவரது தோல்விகளும் பலவீனமான முடிவுகளும் மற்றதை விட சிறப்பாக இல்லை
- சர்வதேச நாணய நிதியத்திற்கு முதல் கடிதத்தை அனுப்பிய நபர் தாம் என்று கூறி இலங்கையின் நிதி நெருக்கடியில் இறங்குவதை மெதுவாக்கத் தவறியதாக பசில் மறுத்தார்.
- பிரதமர் பதவியை பெற்று நாட்டை நெருக்கடியிலிருந்து மீட்பதாக பல தடவைகள் கோரியிருந்த சஜித் பிரேமதாச இன்று அரசாங்கத்தின் மீது விமர்சனங்களை மாத்திரம் முன்வைக்கிறார்.
தற்போதைய பிரதமர் ரணில் விக்கிரமசிங்க, தற்போது இலங்கையின் உடனடி எதிர்காலம் குறித்த இருண்ட படத்தைக் காட்டுவதும் 'திரு. சுத்தம்'

இலங்கை வேறு எங்கும் இல்லாத அளவுக்கு வீழ்ச்சியடைந்த ஒரு நாடு. ஆசியாவின் அதிசயம்', 'இந்தியப் பெருங்கடலின் முத்து', 'முதலீட்டு மையம்', 'பார்க்க வேண்டிய இடம்' என்று ஒரு காலத்தில் அழைக்கப்பட்ட நாடு, இன்று கைகளை விரித்து கெஞ்சுகிறது, அதாவது எரிபொருள், மருந்து, உணவு, அத்தியாவசியப் பொருட்கள் என்று பிச்சை எடுக்கிறது. உலகளாவிய சமூகத்தின் முன்.

யார் எங்களுக்கு உதவ முடியும், நாங்கள் அவர்களிடம் மன்றாடுகிறோம். எங்களின் நிலையைப் பற்றி விசாரிக்கும் உலகளாவிய ஏஜென்சி எதுவாக இருந்தாலும், நாங்கள் அவர்களிடம் விரைந்து செல்கிறோம். 22 மில்லியன் மக்கள் ஒவ்வொரு நாளும் கடந்து செல்லும் வகையில் குறைந்தபட்சம் அடிப்படை வசதிகளுடன் தொடர்ந்து வாழ்வதை உறுதிசெய்வது எங்கள் விரக்தியும் கூட.

பல மாதங்களாக இலங்கை மக்கள் அச்சத்துடனேயே வாழ்ந்து வருகின்றனர். ஒவ்வொரு வீட்டிற்கும் ஒரே ஒரு கதை மட்டுமே உள்ளது - அவர்களின் வீட்டிற்கு எரிவாயு சிலிண்டர் எப்போது வரும்? அவர்களுக்கு மிகவும் தேவையான சில மருந்துகளை எப்போது கிடைக்கும்? அவர்கள் தங்கள் குழந்தைகளுக்கு பால் பவுடர் மற்றும் பிற அத்தியாவசிய பொருட்களை எப்போது வாங்க முடியும்? அவர்கள் எப்போது தங்கள் எரிபொருளை நிரப்ப முடியும் மற்றும் வரிசைகள் எவ்வளவு நேரம் இருக்கும்? மற்றும் மின்சாரம் எப்போது சீராகும்? இப்போது இலங்கையர்களை ஆட்டிப்படைக்கும் அச்சம் கற்பனை செய்ய முடியாதது. ஆனால் அது அவர்கள் இப்போது வாழ வேண்டிய கட்டாயத்தில் உள்ளது.
அப்படியென்றால், ஒருமுறை பூக்கும் இந்த மையம் எப்படி இவ்வளவு அவநம்பிக்கையான தோல்வியடைந்த மாநிலமாக மாறியது?

யார் குற்றம்? எங்கே தவறு செய்தோம்? தவறான நிர்வாகம், திருட்டு, ஊழல், மோசடி, லஞ்சம், கமிஷன், சுயநலம், பேராசை, உறவுமுறை மற்றும் நமது அரசியல்வாதிகள் மற்றும் அரசு அதிகாரிகளின் அதிகார துஷ்பிரயோகம் ஆகியவை ஒட்டுமொத்த தேசத்தையும் துன்பத்தில் ஆழ்த்தியது என்பதில் ஆச்சரியமில்லை. இன்றுவரை இந்த அரசியல்வாதிகள் மற்றும் அதிகாரிகள் யாரும் தங்கள் தவறுகளை ஏற்றுக்கொண்டு பொறுப்பேற்கவில்லை. எந்த தலைவரிடமும் தங்கள் தவறுகளுக்கு மன்னிப்பு கேட்க தேசம் இன்னும் கேட்கவில்லை.

மார்ச் 16 அன்று, ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ச நாட்டு மக்களுக்கு அன்னியச் செலாவணியைக் கண்டுபிடிக்க திறைசேரியைத் துடைத்தபோதும், மின்சார நெருக்கடியின் உச்சக்கட்டத்தில் இருந்தபோதும், தனது பலவீனமான முடிவுகளுக்குப் பொறுப்பேற்க மறுத்ததுதான் மனதில் பதிந்தது. கேமராவை உற்றுப் பார்த்த அவர், அவர் எழுதிய ஸ்கிரிப்டைப் படித்தார். இந்த நெருக்கடியை உடனடியாக தீர்த்து மக்களுக்கு நிவாரணம் வழங்குங்கள்," என்று அவர் கூறினார், அவர் பொது அலுவலகத்திற்கான நியமனங்கள், அவரது முடிவுகள் மற்றும் அவரது முழுமையான கரிம உர பேரழிவுக்கு நெருக்கடியுடன் தொடர்பு இருப்பதை தெளிவாக மறந்துவிட்டார்.

நாட்டின் மிக உயரிய பதவிக்கு தெரிவாகி தற்போது தானும் ஒரு அரசியல்வாதி என்பதை மறந்து விட்டு, இது போன்ற நெருக்கடியான சூழ்நிலையில், ஒரு நாட்டின் அரசியல்வாதிகள் மற்றும் புத்திஜீவிகள் ஒன்றிணைந்து தீர்வு காண்பது பொறுப்பாகும். பிரச்சினைகள். 20ஆவது திருத்தச் சட்டத்தின் கீழ் சகல அதிகாரங்களையும் கொண்ட ஜனாதிபதி என்பதை இங்கும் அவர் மறந்துவிட்டார்.

முன்னாள் பிரதமர் மகிந்த ராஜபக்ச தனது சகோதரரை விட ஒரு மாதத்தின் பின்னர் ஏப்ரல் மாதம் தொலைக்காட்சியில் உரையாற்றிய போது, ​​நாடு மற்றும் எதிர்ப்பாளர்கள் "பொறுமையுடன்" இருக்குமாறு வேண்டுகோள் விடுத்தார், நெருக்கடியால் பாதிக்கப்பட்ட நாடு விலைமதிப்பற்ற டாலர்களை இழக்கிறது என்று கூறினார். தெருக்களில் போராட்டங்கள்.

தனது அரசாங்கத்தின் அப்பட்டமான தோல்விகளுக்கும், தனது அரசாங்கத்திற்கும் மன்னிப்பு கேட்காமல், நீண்ட காலத்திற்கு முன்பு நிதியமைச்சராகப் பணியாற்றியதால், கரிம உரம் எவ்வளவு மரியாதைக்குரியதாக இருந்தாலும், அது நேரம் இல்லை என்பதை பிரதமர் அட்லீஸ்ட் ஏற்றுக்கொண்டார். அதை செயல்படுத்த வேண்டும். எவ்வாறாயினும், அதன்பிறகு அவர் எடுத்த முடிவுகள் அவரது சொந்த சகோதரன் என்ற முறையில் புறக்கணிக்கப்பட்டதாகத் தோன்றியது, அவ்வாறு செய்தால், அவரது தோல்விகள் நன்றாக மறைக்கப்பட்டு மறந்துவிடும் என்று நினைத்து அவரை வெளியேற்றுவதற்கான பிரச்சாரத்தை ஜனாதிபதி தொடங்கினார். மே 9 அன்று, ஒன்பது உயிர்களைப் பலிகொண்ட கொடிய வன்முறைக்குப் பின்னர், மகிந்த ராஜபக்ச இறுதியில் செல்ல வேண்டியிருந்தது.

பின்னர் எங்களிடம் மற்றொரு ராஜபக்ச இருந்தார் - 'கபுடா' என்று அழைக்கப்பட்டவர், அவர் பொருளாதாரத்தின் மீட்பராக இருக்க முடியும் என்று நினைத்து, அவரது சகோதரர் மகிந்த ராஜபக்சவிடம் இருந்து நிதி இலாகாவை வலுக்கட்டாயமாக கைப்பற்றினார் என்று அரசியல் வட்டாரங்கள் கூறுகின்றன. ஆனால் அவரது தோல்விகளும் பலவீனமான முடிவுகளும் மற்றதை விட சிறப்பாக இல்லை. பொருளாதாரம் மேலும் தவறுதலாக உள்ள நிலையில், அண்மையில் நடைபெற்ற ஊடகவியலாளர் சந்திப்பில், எதிர்ப்பாளர்களுக்கு நிவாரணம் அளிக்கும் வகையில், அவர் இறுதியாக பாராளுமன்றத்தில் இருந்து இராஜினாமா செய்தபோது, ​​பசிலுக்கும் எந்த வருத்தமும் இல்லை, உண்மையில் அவர் பந்தை வேறு எவருக்கும் அனுப்பவில்லை என்று கூறினார். நாடு இன்னும் கொஞ்சம் எரிபொருள் பெறுவது அவரால்தான். நிதியமைச்சராகப் பதவியேற்ற பின்னர் சர்வதேச நாணய நிதியத்திற்கு முதல் கடிதம் அனுப்பியவர் என்று கூறி இலங்கையின் நிதிக் கொந்தளிப்பை மெதுவாக்கத் தவறியதை ஒரு முறை பாராளுமன்ற உறுப்பினர் மறுத்தார். "நான் தொடங்கிய பணிதான் இப்போது முன்னோக்கி கொண்டு செல்லப்படுகிறது," என்று அவர் கூறினார், "எனக்கு எந்த வருத்தமும் இல்லை."

ராஜபக்ச மரபைக் கொண்ட ஹம்பாந்தோட்டையில் உள்ள குடும்பங்கள் மற்றும் நாட்டின் ஏனைய அனைத்து மாவட்டங்களும் பொருளாதார வீழ்ச்சிக்கும், வாழ்க்கைச் செலவுக்கும் மத்தியில் பட்டினியுடன் உறங்கும்போது கூட, இந்த மூத்த அரசியல்வாதி இதைச் சொல்வது வெட்கக்கேடானது.

பின்னர் இந்த மாதம் ஒரு வெளிநாட்டு ஊடக நிறுவனத்திற்கு அளித்த பேட்டியில், ஜனாதிபதி ராஜபக்ஷ, தான் ராஜினாமா செய்யப் போவதில்லை என்றும், 'கோட்டா கோ ஹோம்' என்ற பெரும்பான்மை மக்களின் உணர்வுகளை தெளிவாக புறக்கணித்து தனது 5 ஆண்டுகால பதவியை நிறைவு செய்வேன் என்றும் உறுதிப்படுத்தினார்.

"நான் ஒரு தோல்வியுற்ற ஜனாதிபதியாக செல்ல முடியாது," என்று அவர் கூறினார், ஆனால் சர்வதேச நாணய நிதியத்தின் உதவியை நாடுவதற்கு தனது அரசாங்கம் நீண்ட காலம் காத்திருந்ததை ஒப்புக்கொண்டார். குறைந்த பட்சம் ஆறு மாதங்கள் அல்லது ஒரு வருடம் முன்னதாக சென்றிருந்தால், இந்த நிலை வந்திருக்காது. ஆனால் அவருக்கு வாக்களித்த 6.9 மில்லியன் மக்கள் உட்பட அவரது 22 மில்லியன் குடிமக்களிடம் அரச தலைவர் என்ற முறையில் மன்னிப்பு கேட்கவில்லை.

ஆளும் எம்.பி.க்களும், பொது அதிகாரிகளும் மீடியாக்கள், பொதுமக்கள், போராட்டக்காரர்கள் என்று குற்றம் சாட்டுவதில் இருந்து சிறப்பாக இல்லை. ஆனால், தாங்கள் தான் காரணம் என்று அவர்கள் உணர்ந்தார்களா? இல்லை, அவர்கள் தான் பதவிக்கு தேர்ந்தெடுக்கப்பட்டவர்கள் என்பதை மறந்துவிடுங்கள்.

உண்மையில் இன்றுவரை, அரசாங்கத்தில் இருப்பவர்களிடமும், பதவியில் இருந்தவர்களிடமும் நாம் கேட்கும் ஒரே பேச்சு, பழி எப்போதும் வேறொருவர் மீதுதான். ஆனால் அவர்கள் ஒருபோதும் தவறு செய்யவில்லை. இப்போது பொதுமக்களுக்கு அவர்களின் தீர்வு - எரிபொருளைச் சிக்கனமாகப் பயன்படுத்துங்கள், மின்சாரத்தை சிக்கனமாகப் பயன்படுத்துங்கள், குறைவாகச் சாப்பிடுங்கள், சொந்த உணவை வளர்த்துக் கொள்ளுங்கள், மருந்துகளுக்காகக் காத்திருக்க வேண்டும், டாலர்கள் இல்லை, வெளிநாடுகளுக்குச் செலவு செய்வதில் வரம்புகள் இல்லை, குறிப்பாக குழந்தைகளுக்கு அத்தியாவசிய உணவு இல்லை, ஒரு வான ராக்கெட் வாழ்க்கைச் செலவு, எரிவாயு இல்லை மற்றும் மோசமானது இன்னும் வரவில்லை. எந்த ஒரு பொறுப்புணர்ச்சியும் இல்லாமல் ஒவ்வொரு அடியிலும் தியாகம் செய்ய வேண்டிய பொது மக்களுக்கு அது கொதித்தது, அல்லது யார் குற்றம் என்ற பதில்.

எதிர்க்கட்சிகளிடமும் இதைவிட சிறந்த கதை இல்லை. பிரதமர் பதவியை பெற்று நாட்டை நெருக்கடியில் இருந்து விடுவிப்பதற்காக பல தடவைகள் கோரிக்கை விடுத்து வந்த SJB தலைவர் சஜித் பிரேமதாச இன்று அரசாங்கத்தின் மீது விமர்சனங்களை மாத்திரம் சுமத்தி வருகிறார். அவரும் அவரது கட்சியும், தற்போது அவரிடமிருந்தும் அவரது கட்சியிலிருந்தும் பிரிந்து சென்ற சில மூத்த உறுப்பினர்கள், சமூக ஊடகங்களில் தங்களுடைய நாட்களைக் கழிக்கிறார்கள், தவறுகளை முன்னிலைப்படுத்தவும், கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ஷ தெளிவாகத் தெரிவித்த போதிலும், ஜனாதிபதியை வீட்டிற்குச் செல்லுமாறு வலியுறுத்துகிறார்கள். அவரது பதவிக்காலத்தை நிறைவு செய்வார். இந்த எதிர்க்கட்சி உறுப்பினர்கள் அரசாங்கத்திலும் நீண்ட காலத்திற்கு முன்பு இல்லை, எனவே இந்த நெருக்கடியிலிருந்து முற்றிலும் கைகளை கழுவ முடியாது. ஏற்கனவே நலிவடைந்த பொருளாதாரத்தை புதைகுழிக்குள் கொண்டு சென்ற ராஜபக்ஷக்களின் கைகளினால் இந்த கடுமையான நெருக்கடியானது முன்னைய அரசாங்கங்களின் தவறான முகாமைத்துவத்தினால் உருவானது என்பது சாதாரண அறிவுக்கு மட்டுமே.

தற்போதைய பிரதமர் ரணில் விக்கிரமசிங்க, தற்போது இலங்கையின் உடனடி எதிர்காலம் குறித்த இருண்ட படத்தைக் காட்டுவதும் 'திரு. சுத்தம்'. தேசமே கொதிநிலையில் இருக்கும் நேரத்தில் நாட்டைப் பிரதமராகப் பொறுப்பேற்கும் துணிச்சல் அவருக்கு இருந்த போதிலும், ‘பத்திரப்பதிவு ஊழல்’ மூலம் திறைசேரிக்கு கோடிக்கணக்கில் நஷ்டம் ஏற்பட்டபோது, ​​கடந்த அரசாங்கத்திலும் பிரதமராக இருந்தவர் என்பதை நினைவில் கொள்ள வேண்டும். எவ்வாறாயினும், நிவாரணம் மிகவும் தேவைப்படும்போது அவரால் தேசத்திற்கு நிவாரணம் வழங்க முடியுமா என்பது இன்னும் பார்க்கப்படவில்லை.

இலங்கையின் அரசியல்வாதிகள் நீண்டகாலமாக அதன் குடிமக்களின் பொறுமையை சோதித்துள்ளனர். இலஞ்சம், ஊழல், சொந்த பந்தம், மோசடி ஆகியவை நீண்ட காலமாக நாட்டின் அரசியல் வட்டத்தையும் பாராளுமன்றத்தையும் பீடித்துள்ள நிலையில், நாடு நம்மை முன்னெடுத்துச் செல்வதற்கு அதே முகங்களைச் சார்ந்து இருப்பதால் உடனடி நம்பிக்கை இல்லை என்று தோன்றுகிறது. இப்போதைய மக்கள் போராட்டங்கள் அமைப்பை முழுவதுமாக மாற்றுமா என்பது இன்னும் தெரியவில்லை, எதிர்ப்பாளர்களே மறைமுகமான உள்நோக்கங்களுடன் ஊடுருவுபவர்களை விலக்கி வைத்தால், குறிவைக்கப்படக் கூடாதவர்களைத் துன்புறுத்துவதை நிறுத்துங்கள் மற்றும் புவி-அரசியல் நிகழ்ச்சி நிரல்களில் விழ வேண்டாம். ஒரு குரல் - "எங்களுக்கு மாற்றம் வேண்டும்." (ஜமிலா ஹுசைன்)

en.title: SL’s sorry plight - who is to blame?
body_lines: - The nation is yet to hear an apology from any leader for their mistakes.

- On March 16, when President Gotabaya Rajapaksa addressed the nation while the Treasury was scraping to find foreign exchange and at the peak of the power crisis, what stuck to mind was his refusal to take responsibility for any of his weak decisions.

- Then we had another Rajapaksa - the one dubbed as a 'Kaputa' who political sources said took over the Finance portfolio forcefully from his brother Mahinda Rajapaksa, thinking he could be the savior of the economy

- "This crisis was not created by me. When those who contributed to the creation of this crisis are criticizing the government in front of the people today, I am attempting to immediately resolve this crisis" - GR

- Then we had another Rajapaksa - the one dubbed as a 'Kaputa' who political sources said took over the Finance portfolio forcefully from his brother Mahinda Rajapaksa, thinking he could be the savior of the economy. But his failures and weak decisions were no better than the rest
- Basil denied he had failed to slow Sri Lanka’s descent into financial turmoil saying he was the person to send the first letter to the IMF
- Sajith Premadasa who was on multiple occasions requested to take the Premiership and take the country out of crisis, today only hurls criticisms at the government
Current Prime Minister Ranil Wickremesinghe, showing a gloomy picture of Sri Lanka's immediate future presently is also no 'Mr. Clean'

Sri Lanka is a country that has fallen like no other. A country which was once dubbed as the 'Miracle of Asia', 'Pearl Of the Indian Ocean', 'Investment Hub', 'Must See Destination', is today pleading with open hands, literally begging for fuel, medicines, food, essentials in front of the global community.

Whoever can help us, we plead with them. Whatever global agency that even inquires about our status, we rush to them. So is our desperation, to ensure that 22 million people can continue living, with at least the basics so that each day can pass.

For months, Sri Lankans have been surviving in fear. Every household has only one story to tell - when will a gas cylinder arrive in their home? When can they get some much-needed medicines? When can they buy milk powder and other essentials for their children? When can they fill their fuel and how long are the queues? And when will the power normalize? The fear now haunting Sri Lankans is unimaginable. But it is something they are compelled to now live with.
So how did this once-blooming hub turn into such a desperate failed state?

Who is the blame? Where did we go wrong? While the answer is straight, that mismanagement, theft, corruption, fraud, bribes, commissions, selfishness, greed, nepotism and misuse of power by our so-called politicians and public officials resulted in the suffering of an entire nation, it is not surprising that even to date none of these politicians and officials have accepted their faults and taken responsibility. The nation is yet to hear an apology from any leader for their mistakes.

On March 16, when President Gotabaya Rajapaksa addressed the nation while the Treasury was scraping to find foreign exchange and at the peak of the power crisis, what stuck to mind was his refusal to take responsibility for any of his weak decisions. Staring into the camera he read his written script with no regret in his eyes - "This crisis was not created by me. When those who contributed to the creation of this crisis are criticizing the government in front of the people today, I am attempting to immediately resolve this crisis and provide relief to the people," he said, clearly forgetting that his appointments to public office, his decisions, and his utter organic fertilizer disaster had anything to do with the crisis.

Forgetting that he too is now a politician after being elected to the highest seat in the country, he went on to say that in a crisis situation like this, it was the responsibility of the politicians and intellectuals of a country to collectively find solutions to the issues. Here again, he forgot that he was a President who had all powers under the 20th Amendment.

Former Prime Minister Mahinda Rajapaksa did no better than his brother when in a televised address to the nation a month later in April, he appealed to the country and protesters to have “patience” and said that the crisis-hit country was losing precious dollars because of protests on the streets.

Still with no apology whatsoever for his government's utter failures and his own, as he had served as the Finance Minister not too long ago, the Prime Minister atl east accepted that no matter how honourable the notion of organic fertilizer was, it was not the time for it to be implemented. However his decisions thereafter seemed to be ignored as his own brother, the President began a campaign to oust him thinking that if he did so, his failures would be covered up well and forgotten. On May 9, Mahinda Rajapaksa ultimately had to go, after deadly violence which claimed nine lives.

Then we had another Rajapaksa - the one dubbed as a 'Kaputa' who political sources said took over the Finance portfolio forcefully from his brother Mahinda Rajapaksa, thinking he could be the saviour of the economy. But his failures and weak decisions were no better than the rest. With the economy only blundering further, in a recent media briefing when he finally resigned from Parliament much to the relief of the protesters, Basil had no regret too and in fact went on to say that he was not passing the ball onto anyone else but the little fuel the country was still receiving was because of him. This one time MP denied he had failed to slow Sri Lanka’s descent into financial turmoil saying he was the person to send the first letter to the IMF after becoming finance minister. "It is the work I started that is now being taken forward,” he said. “I have no regrets.”

Shame for this senior politician to say this even while families in Hambantota, which holds the Rajapaksa legacy, and all other districts of the country, go to bed hungry amid the economic failures and skyrocketing cost of living.

Then in an interview with a foreign media agency this month, President Rajapaksa confirmed he will not resign and will complete his 5 years in office clearly ignoring the majority’s sentiments of ‘Gota Go Home’.

"I can't go as a failed President," he said but admitted his government had waited too long to seek help from the International Monetary Fund. "If we had gone at least six months or a year earlier, it would not have come to this state," he said. But still no apology as the Head of State to his 22 million citizens including the 6.9 million who voted for him.

The ruling MPs and public officials were no better, from blaming the media, to the public, to the protesters, the ball kept passing. But did they feel they were to blame? No, forgetting that it was they who were elected to office.

In fact even to this date, the only talk we hear from all those in government and who were in office, was that the blame was always on someone else. But they were never the fault. And now their solution to the public - use fuel sparingly, use electricity sparingly, eat less, grow your own food, you will have to wait for medicines, no dollars, limitations on spending overseas, no essential food especially for children, a sky rocketing cost of living, no gas and the worst is yet to come. It then boils down to the public having to sacrifice every step of the way without any accountability, or answers as to who is to blame.

The opposition also does not have a better story. SJB Leader Sajith Premadasa who was on multiple occasions requested to take the Premiership and take the country out of crisis, today only hurls criticisms at the government. He and his party, some senior members who have now broken away from him and his party, spend their days taking to social media and arriving in Parliament just to highlight the faults and insist on the President to go home despite Gotabaya Rajapaksa making it clear he would complete his term. These opposition members were not too long ago in government too, so washing their hands completely away from this crisis cannot do. It is only common sense that this severe crisis has stemmed from the mismanagements of previous governments as well and worsened at the hands of the Rajapaksas, who took the already frail economy into the grave.

Current Prime Minister Ranil Wickremesinghe, showing a gloomy picture of Sri Lanka's immediate future presently is also no 'Mr. Clean'. Although he had the courage to take over the country as the Prime Minister at a time when the nation is on boiling point, should remember that he was also the Prime Minister in the previous government when the 'Bond Scam' cost the Treasury millions. However whether he can bring relief to the nation when relief is needed the most is yet to be seen.

Sri Lanka's politicians have long tested the patience of its citizens. While bribery, corruption, nepotism, fraud have long plagued the country's political circle and Parliament, there seems to be no immediate hope as the country keeps depending on the same faces to take us forward. Whether the current public protests will change the system entirely is yet to be seen, if the protesters themselves keep the infiltrators with ulterior hidden motives away, stop harassing those who should not be targetted and do not fall for geo-political agendas and echo only in one voice - "We want change." (JAMILA HUSAIN)
